# Editor Tools

After capturing a screenshot, RadKit opens an annotation editor in a new tab. The editor provides a canvas-based workspace where you can draw, annotate and export your screenshots.

## Drawing Tools

| Tool | What It Does |
|------|-------------|
| **Pencil** | Draw freehand lines |
| **Line** | Draw straight lines |
| **Arrow** | Draw arrows with directional heads |
| **Rectangle** | Draw rectangular shapes |
| **Circle** | Draw ellipses and circles |
| **Text** | Add text labels directly on the canvas |
| **Blur** | Pixelate a region to hide sensitive information |
| **Image** | Insert an image from a file |

### Using Drawing Tools

1. Select a tool from the toolbar
2. Click and drag on the canvas to draw
3. Release to finish the shape

For the **Text** tool, click to place the text cursor and begin typing. Double-click an existing text element to edit it.

For the **Image** tool, a file picker opens so you can choose an image to insert.

### Customizing Appearance

Each tool remembers its own settings independently. When you switch between tools, your previous color, stroke width and other options are restored.

Available settings:

- **Color**: Choose any color for strokes and fills
- **Stroke width**: Adjust the thickness of lines and shape borders
- **Fill**: Toggle between outlined and filled shapes (rectangles and circles)
- **Font family and size**: Customize text appearance
- **Opacity**: Control element transparency
- **Dash pattern**: Use solid or dashed strokes
- **Shadow**: Add drop shadows to elements

## Crop

Trim your screenshot to a specific area.

1. Select the **Crop** tool
2. Drag to define the crop region
3. Click **Confirm** to apply or **Cancel** to discard

Cropping flattens all visible annotations into the final image before trimming.

## Selecting and Transforming Elements

- Click any element to select it
- Drag to move a selected element
- Use the resize and rotate handles to transform it
- Press **Delete** or **Backspace** to remove the selected element

## Style Presets

Save combinations of color, stroke width and other settings as reusable presets so you can quickly apply your preferred styles across captures.

## Undo and Redo

- **Undo**: Press **Ctrl+Z** (Windows) or **Cmd+Z** (Mac)
- **Redo**: Press **Ctrl+Shift+Z** (Windows) or **Cmd+Shift+Z** (Mac)

## Export Options

| Action | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Copy** | Copy the annotated screenshot to your clipboard |
| **Download** | Save as a PNG file with an automatic file name |
| **Save** | Save as a PNG file with a custom file name |

All exports include the background image and all visible annotations.

## Default Settings

- **Color**: Red
- **Stroke width**: 6 pixels
- **Font size**: 30 pixels (text tool)
